---
title: "Flow Cytometry Analysis: <<TITLE>>"
author: "Claude + EYW"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 4
    theme: flatly
    highlight: default
    code_folding: hide
    fig_width: 14
    fig_height: 6
---

<!--
TEMPLATE USAGE
  Copy this file into the project root as flow_cytometry_analysis_<<EXPERIMENT>>.Rmd, then adapt:
    1. Set root.dir below to the project directory.
    2. Fill the USER PARAMETERS block (set EXPERIMENT first — it is appended to
       every output: plot PDFs, processed CSVs, and the report title).
    3. Wire real column names / regexes into the reshape chunks
       (see references/flowjo-csv-format.md).
    4. Duplicate the analysis section once per measurement family
       (see references/plotting-patterns.md).
  Replace every <<PLACEHOLDER>>. Delete sections you don't need (e.g. QC).
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = '<<ABSOLUTE/PROJECT/PATH/>>')
```

# Overview

<<One paragraph: what is being compared, which markers/analytes, how fold-change is defined
(per replicate vs the control level).>>

---

# 1 · Setup

```{r libraries}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyplots)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
# Use tidyplots' default theme — do NOT source plotting_fxns.R / theme_Publication().

# ── USER PARAMETERS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EXPERIMENT <- "<<experiment_slug>>"  # filename-safe label appended to ALL outputs
                                     # (plot PDFs, processed CSVs, report title)
CTRL_LEVEL <- "<<none>>"          # control level for fold-change (e.g. "none", "DMSO")
X_VAR      <- "<<cytokine>>"      # x-axis factor (metadata column name)
COLOR_VAR  <- "<<treatment>>"    # comparison / color factor (metadata column name)
FACET_VAR  <- "<<marker>>"       # faceting variable (the analyte; created during reshape)

X_LEVELS     <- c(<<"IL2","IL4",...,"none">>)   # x-axis order; put CTRL_LEVEL where it belongs
COLOR_LEVELS <- c(<<"VLP","recomb">>)            # comparison order
GEOMETRY     <- "bar"                            # "bar" (means) | "violin" | "box"

COLORS <- c(<<VLP = "#1b9e77", recomb = "#d95f02">>)  # named per COLOR_LEVELS

# ── Panel size & facet grid ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# One source of truth for plot size: PANEL_W/PANEL_H (mm) are fed to adjust_size()
# (which sizes the PDF), and fig_dim() derives the knitr chunk fig.width/fig.height
# (inches, which size the HTML figure) from the SAME panel dims and facet grid — so the
# two never drift. Change a panel size, NCOL, or the number of markers and both the PDF
# and the embedded HTML figure rescale together. Bump PANEL_W if x-axis bars look cramped;
# raise width_scale in fig_dim() if the HTML figure itself looks squished (legend clipped).
PANEL_W <- 60    # per-panel width  (mm) — fed to adjust_size()
PANEL_H <- 40    # per-panel height (mm)
NCOL    <- <<N>> # facet columns; rows are derived from the marker count

fig_dim <- function(n_panels, ncol = NCOL, w_mm = PANEL_W, h_mm = PANEL_H,
                    legend_in = 1.3, title_in = 0.6,
                    width_scale = 1.25, height_scale = 1.25) {
  nrow <- ceiling(n_panels / ncol)
  # mm -> inches (/25.4); pad width for the color legend and height for the title.
  # width_scale / height_scale give the chunk device extra room so the HTML figure isn't
  # squished relative to the plot's true size (panels + legend + axis titles).
  c(w = (ncol * w_mm / 25.4 + legend_in) * width_scale,
    h = (nrow * h_mm / 25.4 + title_in) * height_scale)
}

# ── PDF export ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Every plot is written to PLOT_DIR as a PDF *and* shown in the report. In each plot
# chunk: assign the finished plot to `p`, call save_plot() on it (which honours the
# physical size set by adjust_size()), then put `p` on its own line so knitr embeds it.
# IMPORTANT: pass view_plot = FALSE to save_plot(). It defaults to TRUE, whose final
# print(input) draws the plot to the active device — knitr captures that AND auto-prints
# the returned `p`, embedding every figure in the HTML twice. Give each plot a short,
# unique, filename-safe name; append the EXPERIMENT slug so analyses never overwrite
# each other, e.g.:
#   save_plot(p, file.path(PLOT_DIR, paste0("<name>__", EXPERIMENT, ".pdf")), view_plot = FALSE)
PLOT_DIR <- "plots"
dir.create(PLOT_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
```

---

# 2 · Data Loading & Wrangling

## 2.1 Load raw files

```{r load}
metadata_raw <- read_csv("tables/metadata.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
# One read_csv per measurement CSV:
meas_raw     <- read_csv("tables/<<measurements>>.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
```

## 2.2 Derive constants & colors

```{r derive-constants}
# Auto-fill colors for any comparison level not explicitly named above.
new_lv <- setdiff(COLOR_LEVELS, names(COLORS))
if (length(new_lv)) {
  auto   <- setdiff(RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(8, "Set2"), unname(COLORS))
  COLORS <- c(COLORS, setNames(auto[seq_along(new_lv)], new_lv))
}
cat("x-axis   :", paste(X_LEVELS,     collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("compare  :", paste(COLOR_LEVELS, collapse = ", "), "\n")
```

## 2.3 Clean metadata

```{r clean-meta}
metadata <- metadata_raw %>%
  rename(plate = GROUPNAME, well = `$WELLID`) %>%
  mutate(
    replicate = as.integer(replicate),
    !!X_VAR     := factor(.data[[X_VAR]],     levels = X_LEVELS),
    !!COLOR_VAR := factor(.data[[COLOR_VAR]], levels = COLOR_LEVELS)
    # cast any per-group analyte column to a factor here too.
  )
```

## 2.4 Reshape measurements to long

```{r reshape}
# "n/a" channels must become NA before any numeric op (see flowjo-csv-format.md).
parse_num <- function(x) as.numeric(na_if(as.character(x), "n/a"))

# Gating hierarchy from a FlowJo column header: the text before " | " is the
# slash-delimited gate path; gate_level() pulls the nth population from the leaf end
# (n = 1 = leaf), returning NA past the root. Capture parent / grandparent so the data
# can be plotted against gating context (e.g. the "Freq. of Parent" denominator) later.
# For a Freq column the leaf IS the population, so its parent is gate_level(.,2);
# for an MFI column the leaf is the gate the stat sits on, so its parent is gate_level(.,1).
extract_gate <- function(col) str_trim(str_extract(col, "^[^|]+"))
gate_level   <- function(path, n) str_split_i(path, fixed("/"), -n)

# ── Pattern A: every column is a marker of the same family (e.g. all frequencies) ──
df_long <- meas_raw %>%
  rename(plate = GROUPNAME, well = `$WELLID`) %>%
  select(-`Sample:`) %>%
  pivot_longer(-c(plate, well), names_to = "col_name", values_to = "value",
               values_transform = list(value = as.character)) %>%
  mutate(
    value       = parse_num(value),
    marker      = str_trim(str_extract(col_name, "<<(?<=/)([^/|]+)(?=\\s*\\| Freq)>>")),
    gate_path   = extract_gate(col_name),       # full gate path incl. the population leaf
    population  = gate_level(gate_path, 1),      # the gated population (== marker for Freq)
    parent      = gate_level(gate_path, 2),      # Freq. of Parent denominator
    grandparent = gate_level(gate_path, 3)       # one level above the parent
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(marker)) %>%
  inner_join(metadata, by = c("plate", "well"))

# ── Pattern B: one analyte per group; pick the right value per row by metadata ──
# Use case_when() on a metadata column (e.g. fluorophore) to select among stat columns,
# OR inner_join on the analyte-naming metadata column. See flowjo-csv-format.md.
# For MFI columns the gate path has no population leaf, so capture hierarchy with:
#   gate_path = extract_gate(<the stat column name>),
#   parent = gate_level(gate_path, 1), grandparent = gate_level(gate_path, 2)
# Keep gate_path / parent / grandparent in the final select() so they reach the
# processed CSV and are available for parent/grandparent-population plots.

# ── Pattern C: counts → absolute cell numbers (see flowjo-csv-format.md) ──
# Counts exports carry a per-sample $VOL index column (µL acquired). Keep it OUT of the
# pivot (rename to `vol`), then after the metadata join convert raw events to absolute
# cell number with the volume calc. Plot/normalize abs_count, not the raw value.
# CONFIRM THE VOLUME MAPPING WITH THE USER FIRST (SKILL.md step 3) — a mislabeled volume
# produces plausible-looking but wrong absolute counts. Default meanings:
#   $VOL               = acquired volume (volume the cytometer actually ran; from counts CSV)
#   flow_vol           = volume resuspended for flow cytometry
#   stained_sample_vol = volume of sample used for staining
#   total_sample_vol   = volume entire sample is resuspended in
# counts_long <- counts_raw %>%
#   rename(plate = GROUPNAME, well = `$WELLID`, vol = `$VOL`) %>%
#   select(-`Sample:`) %>%
#   pivot_longer(-c(plate, well, vol), names_to = "col_name", values_to = "value",
#                values_transform = list(value = as.character)) %>%
#   mutate(value      = parse_num(value),
#          marker     = str_trim(str_extract(col_name, "(?<=/)([^/|]+)(?=\\s*\\| Count)")),
#          gate_path  = extract_gate(col_name),
#          population = gate_level(gate_path, 1),   # leaf gate == counted population
#          parent     = gate_level(gate_path, 2)) %>%
#   filter(!is.na(marker)) %>%
#   inner_join(metadata, by = c("plate", "well")) %>%
#   mutate(abs_count = value * (flow_vol / vol) * (total_sample_vol / stained_sample_vol))
# For counts sections, use y = abs_count in the plot chains below and key fold-change on it.

df_long %>% count(marker) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Row counts per marker") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("condensed", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
```

## 2.5 Fold-change vs control

```{r fold-change}
# Key on everything EXCEPT the normalization axis (here, X_VAR holds CTRL_LEVEL).
fc_keys  <- c("marker", COLOR_VAR, "replicate")

baseline <- df_long %>%
  filter(.data[[X_VAR]] == CTRL_LEVEL) %>%
  select(all_of(fc_keys), baseline = value)

df_long <- df_long %>%
  left_join(baseline, by = fc_keys) %>%
  mutate(fold_change = value / baseline,
         log2FC      = log2(fold_change)) %>%
  select(-baseline)
```

```{r write-processed, include=FALSE}
df_long %>% write_csv(paste0("tables/<<measurements>>_processed__", EXPERIMENT, ".csv"))
```

## 2.6 Summaries

```{r summarise}
sem <- function(x) sd(x, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(x)))

summ <- df_long %>%
  group_by(.data[[COLOR_VAR]], .data[[X_VAR]], marker) %>%
  summarise(n = sum(!is.na(value)),
            mean_v = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), sem_v = sem(value),
            mean_fc = mean(fold_change, na.rm = TRUE), sem_fc = sem(fold_change),
            .groups = "drop")
```

---

# 3 · <<Measurement family>> {.tabset}

<!-- A small helper keeps the three subtabs DRY. Geometry is switched by GEOMETRY. -->

```{r plot-helper}
add_geometry <- function(p) {
  if (GEOMETRY == "violin") p %>% add_violin() %>% add_data_points()
  else if (GEOMETRY == "box") p %>% add_boxplot() %>% add_data_points()
  else p %>% add_mean_bar(alpha = 0.7) %>% add_sd_errorbar() %>% add_data_points()
}

# Figure dims for this family, derived from the marker count + NCOL. The plot chunks below
# reference it in their headers (fig.width = DIM["w"], fig.height = DIM["h"]).
DIM <- fig_dim(n_distinct(df_long$marker))
```

## 3.1 Raw

```{r raw, fig.width=DIM["w"], fig.height=DIM["h"]}
p <- df_long %>%
  tidyplot(x = .data[[X_VAR]], y = value, color = .data[[COLOR_VAR]]) %>%
  add_geometry() %>%
  adjust_colors(new_colors = COLORS) %>%
  adjust_title("<<Metric>> — Raw") %>%
  adjust_x_axis_title("<<x label>>") %>%
  adjust_y_axis_title("<<value>> ± SD") %>%
  split_plot(by = marker, ncol = NCOL) %>%
  adjust_size(width = PANEL_W, height = PANEL_H)
save_plot(p, file.path(PLOT_DIR, paste0("<<metric>>_raw__", EXPERIMENT, ".pdf")), view_plot = FALSE)
p
```

## 3.2 Fold change vs `r CTRL_LEVEL`

```{r fc, fig.width=DIM["w"], fig.height=DIM["h"]}
p <- df_long %>%
  filter(.data[[X_VAR]] != CTRL_LEVEL) %>%
  tidyplot(x = .data[[X_VAR]], y = fold_change, color = .data[[COLOR_VAR]]) %>%
  add_reference_lines(y = 1) %>%
  add_geometry() %>%
  adjust_colors(new_colors = COLORS) %>%
  adjust_title("<<Metric>> — Fold change vs control") %>%
  adjust_x_axis_title("<<x label>>") %>%
  adjust_y_axis_title("Fold change ± SD") %>%
  split_plot(by = marker, ncol = NCOL) %>%
  adjust_size(width = PANEL_W, height = PANEL_H)
save_plot(p, file.path(PLOT_DIR, paste0("<<metric>>_fold_change__", EXPERIMENT, ".pdf")), view_plot = FALSE)
p
```

## 3.3 log2 Fold change vs `r CTRL_LEVEL`

```{r log2fc, fig.width=DIM["w"], fig.height=DIM["h"]}
p <- df_long %>%
  filter(.data[[X_VAR]] != CTRL_LEVEL) %>%
  tidyplot(x = .data[[X_VAR]], y = log2FC, color = .data[[COLOR_VAR]]) %>%
  add_reference_lines(y = 0) %>%
  add_geometry() %>%
  adjust_colors(new_colors = COLORS) %>%
  adjust_title("<<Metric>> — log2 fold change vs control") %>%
  adjust_x_axis_title("<<x label>>") %>%
  adjust_y_axis_title("log2(FC) ± SD") %>%
  split_plot(by = marker, ncol = NCOL) %>%
  adjust_size(width = PANEL_W, height = PANEL_H)
save_plot(p, file.path(PLOT_DIR, paste0("<<metric>>_log2FC__", EXPERIMENT, ".pdf")), view_plot = FALSE)
p
```

<!-- Duplicate section 3 for each additional measurement family. -->

---

# 4 · Session Info

```{r session}
sessionInfo()
```
